title: U.S. Mission to NATO Grants Program 2025: Funding Opportunities to Advance Transatlantic Defense, Security, and Industrial Cooperation
summary: The U.S. Mission to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (USNATO) has announced funding opportunities under its Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program, designed to strengthen transatlantic defense cooperation, support NATO priorities, and reinforce U.S. leadership within the Alliance. Organizations, institutions, researchers, policy experts, civil society actors, and industry stakeholders working in areas related to defense, security, and industrial collaboration are encouraged to apply. Located at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium, the U.S. Mission to NATO serves as the official representation of the United States to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization. Through diplomatic engagement, policy coordination, public diplomacy, and strategic partnerships, the mission works to advance peace, collective defense, and security cooperation among NATO member states and partner countries. The grants program reflects the United States’ commitment to building a stronger, safer, and more prosperous Alliance by supporting innovative initiatives that contribute to NATO’s collective defense and industrial capacity. About the U.S. Mission to NATO The U.S. Mission to NATO represents U.S. foreign policy interests within the Alliance and plays a central role in diplomatic coordination among NATO member countries. The Mission supports engagement at the highest political and military levels through the North Atlantic Council (NAC), NATO’s principal political decision-making body. The NAC brings together representatives from all NATO member states to discuss and coordinate responses to political, defense, and operational security matters affecting the Alliance. The U.S. Permanent Representative to NATO occupies the U.S. chair in the NAC and oversees diplomatic representation within NATO structures. Key Officers at the U.S. Mission to NATO The leadership team at the U.S. Mission to NATO includes: Matthew Whitaker – Permanent Representative of the United States to NATO Scott Oudkirk – Deputy Chief of Mission Scott Delius – Senior Civilian Representative of the Secretary of War in Europe and Defense Advisor Stephen Gee – Political Advisor Aaron Honn – Public Affairs Advisor These officials help coordinate U.S. engagement on defense, diplomacy, strategic cooperation, and public affairs initiatives across the NATO Alliance. Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program The Office of the Public Affairs Advisor manages the Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program. The initiative supports projects and partnerships that advance key U.S. and NATO priorities across: Academia Public policy Defense and security sectors Industry collaboration Civil society organizations Strategic research institutions The grants program prioritizes projects that: Reinforce U.S. leadership within NATO Advance defense investment and industrial capacity Strengthen transatlantic security cooperation Build professional expertise and strategic networks Translate NATO commitments into practical outcomes Support a stronger and more resilient Alliance The program is designed to encourage innovative ideas and collaborative solutions that improve collective defense readiness and deepen international cooperation among NATO member states and partner countries. Funding Opportunity: Advancing NATO’s Collective Defense and Industrial Capacity One of the highlighted funding opportunities currently available through the U.S. Mission to NATO is: Advancing NATO’s Collective Defense and Industrial Capacity Funding Opportunity Number: OFOP0002706 This funding opportunity focuses on initiatives that contribute to NATO’s operational readiness, defense infrastructure, industrial resilience, and long-term security cooperation among allied nations. Applicants are encouraged to develop impactful proposals that align with NATO’s strategic priorities and support sustainable transatlantic partnerships. Eligible Areas of Support Projects supported under the grants program may include: Defense and security policy research NATO awareness and educational initiatives Strategic dialogue forums and workshops Industrial cooperation and defense innovation projects Capacity-building activities Public diplomacy and outreach campaigns Academic conferences and policy engagement programs Networking initiatives among experts and institutions Collaborative research on collective defense Programs that demonstrate measurable impact, international collaboration, and alignment with NATO priorities are especially encouraged. Application Guidelines Applicants must carefully follow all application requirements and submission instructions. Important Guidelines All application forms must be completed in English. Budgets and financial figures must be converted into U.S. dollars (USD). Programs involving travel to or from the United States must comply with the Fly America Act. Successful grantees will work closely with assigned Grants Officers and Grants Officer Representatives to ensure proper implementation and allowable program costs. Applicants are advised to prepare detailed proposals that clearly explain: Project goals Expected outcomes Target audience Budget breakdown Timeline Strategic relevance to NATO priorities Where to Apply All available grant opportunities offered by the U.S. Mission to NATO are posted online through Grants.gov to ensure transparency and open competition. Applicants should regularly monitor the official portal for updates, application requirements, and deadlines. Explore grants and apply here Official Residence: Truman Hall The official residence associated with the U.S. Mission to NATO is Truman Hall, a historic Flemish country estate located in Belgium. Built in 1963 for Belgian chocolatier Jean Michiels of Côte d’Or, the estate later became the residence of the U.S. Ambassador to NATO. Named after President Harry S. Truman, one of NATO’s founders, the residence symbolizes the enduring partnership between the United States and Europe. Features of Truman Hall The estate includes: Expansive formal gardens Landscaped meadows and valleys English gardens and herb gardens A pavilion surrounded by flowering plants Historic architectural features Belgian black marble interiors A library with 18th-century wood paneling The landscape design was created by renowned architect René Pechère, who transformed agricultural land into a scenic diplomatic residence welcoming visitors from NATO member and partner countries. A special commemorative garden featuring cobbled circles representing NATO countries was added in 1984. Truman Hall remains an important symbol of diplomacy, cooperation, and transatlantic partnership. Art in Embassies Program The U.S. Department of State’s Art in Embassies (AIE) program also plays a significant role at the U.S. Mission to NATO. Established in 1963, the program promotes cultural diplomacy by curating exhibitions and artistic installations in U.S. embassies, consulates, and diplomatic residences worldwide. The NATO permanent collection honors: The United States The U.S. Armed Forces Shared democratic values International cultural exchange Through art and cultural engagement, the program strengthens mutual understanding among nations and highlights the diversity of American and international artistic expression. Why This Opportunity Matters The U.S. Mission to NATO grants initiative offers an important platform for organizations and institutions seeking to contribute to international security, defense cooperation, strategic policy development, and industrial resilience. The program provides opportunities to: Expand transatlantic partnerships Influence defense and security dialogue Support NATO priorities Build institutional capacity Engage with international policymakers and experts Contribute to global peace and collective defense efforts For institutions focused on international relations, security studies, defense policy, innovation, diplomacy, and public engagement, this funding opportunity can serve as a major catalyst for impactful projects and international collaboration. Deadline No specific application deadline was stated in the provided information. Applicants are encouraged to check the official Grants.gov portal regularly for updated submission timelines and funding notices. For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more grant opportunities here The post U.S. Mission to NATO Grants Program 2025: Funding Opportunities to Advance Transatlantic Defense, Security, and Industrial Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: InteRussia Fellowship for International Relations Specialists 2026 in Russia | Fully Funded Research Fellowship
summary: The InteRussia Fellowship for International Relations Specialists 2026 is now accepting applications from qualified foreign researchers, political scientists, regional studies experts, and international relations professionals from around the world. This prestigious international fellowship offers participants a rare opportunity to immerse themselves in Russian academia, engage directly with policy-making institutions, and collaborate with leading scholars at MGIMO University in Moscow, Russia. The fellowship is designed for emerging and mid-career experts who are passionate about international relations, political science, regional studies, and global geopolitical developments. Hosted in collaboration with MGIMO Institute for International Studies and the Gorchakov Fund, the program aims to strengthen academic dialogue and promote global cooperation in an increasingly multipolar world. The 2026 edition of the InteRussia Fellowship will focus on the theme: “The World Divided? The Current Trends in Military-Political Alignment and Economic Integration” The program will run from October 26 to December 6, 2026 , in Moscow, Russia , and will be conducted entirely in English . About the InteRussia Fellowship Program The InteRussia Fellowship is an international scientific and research program created to connect talented scholars and experts from different countries with Russia’s leading academic and policy institutions. Participants will gain first-hand exposure to Russian perspectives on global politics, international cooperation, regional integration, and contemporary geopolitical challenges. Through lectures, seminars, expert sessions, and institutional visits, fellows will build professional networks and deepen their understanding of global affairs. A major highlight of the fellowship is the opportunity to work under the supervision and mentorship of leading Russian scholars from MGIMO University, one of the most prestigious institutions in international relations and diplomacy. The fellowship seeks to foster: International academic collaboration Cross-cultural dialogue Research cooperation New perspectives in global governance Networks among emerging global experts Fellowship Theme for 2026 The research theme for the 2026 fellowship is: “The World Divided? The Current Trends in Military-Political Alignment and Economic Integration” Applicants are expected to submit a research proposal connected to this theme. Selected fellows will later produce an original individual research paper based on their proposal. The final research project must contain: 15,000–25,000 characters (including spaces) Original academic analysis Research written in English Content aligned with the submitted proposal Fellowship Location and Duration Host Country: Russia City: Moscow Program Dates: October 26 – December 6, 2026 Language: English What Participants Will Experience Selected fellows will benefit from a highly immersive academic and professional experience. Activities during the fellowship include: Academic Engagement Participants will: Attend expert discussions and academic sessions Participate in seminars and workshops Collaborate with scholars and researchers Conduct research under expert supervision Institutional Exposure Fellows will have opportunities to: Visit Russian policy-making institutions Engage with think tanks and NGOs Explore Russian approaches to international relations Learn directly from Russian experts and practitioners International Networking Participants will: Connect with peers from different countries Build international professional relationships Exchange ideas on emerging global challenges Join a growing global network of scholars Financial Benefits of the InteRussia Fellowship The organizers will provide financial support covering several major expenses. Covered Benefits The fellowship organizers will cover: Round-trip travel to and from Moscow Single accommodation in the MGIMO dormitory A one-time scholarship payment of RUB 45,000 for the duration of the fellowship Expenses Not Covered Participants will be responsible for: Visa application fees Medical insurance Meals during the fellowship period Applicants should plan their budgets accordingly before applying. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the InteRussia Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet all the following requirements: Citizenship and Residency Applicants must: Be non-Russian citizens Reside outside Russia Age Requirement Applicants must be between: 25 and 39 years old Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate: English proficiency at C1 level or higher Academic and Professional Requirements Applicants must meet at least one of the following: Hold a PhD degree Have at least 3 years of experience in academic institutions or think tanks Be currently pursuing PhD studies Additional Requirement Applicants must: Have no employment history or academic enrollment with MGIMO University Submit a detailed research proposal Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated competitively based on several important factors. The organizers will consider: Quality, relevance, feasibility, and detail of the research proposal Academic publications Recommendation letters (2–3 required) Participation in conferences, grants, fellowships, and research activities Expert publications and media contributions Performance during the online interview Strong academic and research backgrounds will significantly improve an applicant’s chances. Required Application Materials Applicants should prepare the following: Research proposal Academic CV Recommendation letters Proof of academic or professional experience Supporting publications and research activities How to Apply for the InteRussia Fellowship 2026 Interested candidates should follow these application steps carefully: Step 1: Register Create an account on the English version of the Gorchakov Fund website. Step 2: Access “My Projects” After registration: Go to “My Projects” Click “Create Application” Step 3: Select the Fellowship Program Choose: “InteRussia IR MGIMO 2026 (Autumn)” Then confirm your selection. Step 4: Complete the Application Form Fill in all mandatory fields carefully and upload required documents. Step 5: Submit the Application Click the “Submit Application” button and ensure your application status changes from: “Preparing” to “Submitted” Apply here Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the InteRussia Fellowship for International Relations Specialists 2026 is: September 1, 2026 at 11:59 PM Moscow Time (UTC +3) Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. Why You Should Apply The InteRussia Fellowship offers a valuable platform for researchers and international affairs professionals seeking global exposure and academic collaboration. Key reasons to apply include: Fully funded international academic experience Research mentorship from top scholars Access to Russian policy and academic institutions International networking opportunities Career advancement in international relations and political science Opportunity to publish and strengthen research credentials Organizing Institutions The InteRussia Fellowship Program in International Relations is implemented by: Mezhdunarodniki Autonomous Non-Profit Organisation Gorchakov Fund MGIMO Institute for International Studies The program is supported through a grant from the Presidential Grants Foundation. Official Contact Information For inquiries regarding the fellowship, applicants may contact: Email: info@interussia.com Phone: +7 (495) 276-16-62 Applicants are encouraged to review all application requirements carefully before submission. For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more fellowship opportunities here. The post InteRussia Fellowship for International Relations Specialists 2026 in Russia | Fully Funded Research Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: KIC 2026 AgriTech Challenge Pro Cohort: Empowering Ghana’s Next Generation of Agribusiness Innovators
summary: The Kosmos Innovation Center (KIC) has officially opened applications for the 2026 AgriTech Challenge Pro Cohort, an advanced business incubation program designed to support innovative agribusiness startups in Ghana. The initiative seeks to empower young entrepreneurs who are building transformative solutions in agriculture, food innovation, and agri-machinery by equipping them with the tools, mentorship, technical expertise, and market opportunities needed to scale sustainable businesses. As Ghana’s agricultural sector continues to evolve, innovation and technology are increasingly becoming critical drivers of productivity, food security, employment creation, and economic transformation. The AgriTech Challenge Pro was created to bridge the gap between innovative ideas and commercially viable agribusiness ventures by providing structured incubation support and access to world-class innovation resources. This opportunity is ideal for ambitious startups that are ready to refine their products, expand their market reach, prepare for investment opportunities, and contribute meaningful solutions to challenges within the agricultural value chain. About the KIC 2026 AgriTech Challenge Pro Cohort The AgriTech Challenge Pro is an advanced incubation and acceleration program focused on helping high-potential agribusiness startups move from the development stage to commercialization and scale. The program particularly targets startups operating in areas such as: Food Innovation Agri-Machinery Agricultural Technology Solutions Agribusiness Innovation Value Chain Enhancement Smart Farming Solutions Agricultural Engineering and Mechanization Selected participants will work closely with industry professionals, business advisors, technical experts, and experienced mentors to accelerate the growth of their businesses and bring innovative agricultural solutions to market faster. The program aims to support startups that are committed to using technology and innovation to: Enhance agricultural productivity Improve yields Solve critical agricultural challenges Create sustainable jobs Strengthen Ghana’s agribusiness ecosystem Drive economic transformation within the agricultural sector What Makes the AgriTech Challenge Pro Unique? One of the most outstanding aspects of the AgriTech Challenge Pro is the access participants receive to KIC’s Center of Excellence in Kumasi. The Center of Excellence is a highly specialized innovation facility that provides advanced technical infrastructure, engineering support, commercialization services, and maker spaces tailored for startups, MSMEs, and corporate partners. The facility consists of six specialized maker centers that help innovators transform ideas into practical, market-ready agricultural technologies and products. This means participants will not only receive theoretical business support but will also gain hands-on access to technical resources needed to prototype, refine, test, and improve their innovations. Benefits of the KIC AgriTech Challenge Pro Selected startups and entrepreneurs will gain access to several valuable opportunities and support systems, including: 1. Business Incubation and Acceleration Support Participants will receive structured guidance to strengthen business models, operational strategies, and long-term growth plans. 2. Industry Mentorship and Coaching Entrepreneurs will interact with industry leaders, experienced mentors, and successful agribusiness professionals who will provide strategic direction and practical insights. 3. Product and Market Development Support Startups will receive support in refining products, identifying market opportunities, improving customer readiness, and preparing solutions for commercialization. 4. Access to Technical Expertise and Innovation Resources Participants will benefit from KIC’s advanced innovation infrastructure, engineering support, and technical facilities. 5. Investor and Ecosystem Linkages The program connects startups with investors, ecosystem stakeholders, and potential strategic partners to support business growth and sustainability. 6. Entrepreneurial Capacity Building Participants will gain critical entrepreneurial skills in leadership, innovation management, business development, and scaling strategies. 7. Networking and Collaboration Opportunities The cohort creates opportunities for collaboration among innovators, entrepreneurs, industry experts, and agribusiness stakeholders. Supporting Youth Transformation Through Agriculture The AgriTech Challenge Pro forms part of the Initiative for Youth in Agricultural Transformation (IYAT), a strategic initiative by the Mastercard Foundation in partnership with Kosmos Innovation Center (KIC) . The initiative focuses on equipping the next generation of young leaders and entrepreneurs in Ghana’s agricultural sector with the knowledge, resources, and opportunities required to drive innovation and unlock growth barriers across key agricultural value chains. The partnership also aims to position Ghana as a continental example of agricultural innovation tailored to the African context. Since its inception, the initiative has reportedly contributed to creating more than 64,165 direct and indirect jobs for young people. Why Young Agribusiness Innovators Should Apply The agricultural sector remains one of the largest contributors to employment and economic growth in Ghana and across Africa. However, many agricultural challenges still require innovative and scalable solutions. The KIC AgriTech Challenge Pro provides a rare opportunity for startups to gain: Technical support Business mentorship Access to infrastructure Industry exposure Commercialization guidance Investor readiness support Strategic partnerships For entrepreneurs who are serious about scaling their agribusiness innovations and making measurable impact within the agricultural ecosystem, this program can serve as a major catalyst for growth. Who Should Apply? The program is suitable for: Early-stage agribusiness startups Growth-stage agritech companies Young innovators in agriculture Entrepreneurs developing food innovation solutions Startups focused on agri-machinery and mechanization Technology-driven agricultural ventures Businesses solving challenges across the agricultural value chain Applicants should demonstrate innovation potential, scalability, and commitment to creating sustainable impact within Ghana’s agriculture sector. Application Deadline No official application deadline was stated in the announcement at the time of publication. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before applications close. How to Apply Interested entrepreneurs and startups can learn more and apply through the official KIC website: Apply here The deadline for submission of the application is May 24, 2026. Final Thoughts The KIC 2026 AgriTech Challenge Pro Cohort represents a significant opportunity for Ghanaian agribusiness innovators to access high-level incubation support, technical infrastructure, mentorship, and market opportunities. As agriculture increasingly becomes driven by technology and innovation, programs like the AgriTech Challenge Pro are helping shape the future of agribusiness in Ghana by empowering young entrepreneurs to create scalable, impactful, and sustainable solutions. Entrepreneurs ready to transform agriculture through innovation should strongly consider applying for this highly impactful program. For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more accelerator programs here The post KIC 2026 AgriTech Challenge Pro Cohort: Empowering Ghana’s Next Generation of Agribusiness Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Communication and Advocacy Assistant Opportunity in Germany (Remote): Fully Detailed Guide for Global Youth Communication Professionals
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), through the United Nations Volunteers (UNV) programme, is inviting applications for the position of Communication and Advocacy Assistant under its Global Youth Team. This exciting international opportunity is designed for passionate professionals with experience in communications, digital storytelling, youth engagement, advocacy, social media management, and visual content creation. The role is linked to UNDP’s growing global youth empowerment agenda and the internationally recognized Generation17 initiative , a flagship collaboration between UNDP and Samsung that supports young leaders working toward the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This is a full-time remote UN Volunteer assignment based in Bonn, Germany, with a duration of 12 months and the possibility of extension. The role offers candidates the chance to contribute to global campaigns, digital advocacy, youth participation initiatives, and high-impact international development programmes. Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 About UNDP and the Global Youth Team The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) operates in approximately 170 countries and territories worldwide. As the UN’s development agency, UNDP works to eradicate poverty, reduce inequality, strengthen democratic governance, promote peacebuilding, and help countries achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Within this broader mission, youth empowerment has become a central priority. UNDP views young people not merely as beneficiaries of development programmes but as leaders, innovators, and changemakers capable of driving sustainable transformation globally. The UNDP Global Youth Team functions under the: Open and Inclusive Public Sphere (OIPS) Team Governance, Rule of Law and Peacebuilding (GRP) Hub Bureau for Policy and Programme Support (BPPS) The team works across: Global platforms Regional initiatives Country-level programmes Its mission includes: Promoting inclusive youth participation in governance Strengthening youth leadership Supporting youth-led innovation Enhancing civic engagement Advancing peacebuilding and digital participation Amplifying the voices of marginalized and underrepresented youth The team collaborates closely with: The UN Youth Office UN agencies Civil society organizations Youth-led organizations Governments International partners Understanding the Generation17 Initiative One of the most exciting aspects of this assignment is the opportunity to contribute to Generation17 , a flagship partnership between UNDP and Samsung Mobile. Launched in 2020, Generation17 empowers young leaders worldwide who are creating innovative solutions aligned with the SDGs. The initiative provides: Mentorship opportunities International advocacy platforms Access to technology Global networking Digital storytelling support Visibility for youth-led impact initiatives Generation17 also connects with the Samsung Global Goals App, installed on millions of Samsung Galaxy devices globally. The app helps educate users about the SDGs while enabling fundraising and awareness-building through everyday mobile engagement. To date, UNDP and Samsung have supported young leaders from multiple regions across the world. Position Overview Job Title Communication and Advocacy Assistant Organization UNDP (United Nations Development Programme) Location Bonn, Germany Work Arrangement Remote / Onsite Modality Duration 12 Months (with possibility of extension) Work Schedule Full-time Volunteer Category Associate UCoS from April 2026 Number of Openings 1 Assignment Main Responsibilities of the Communication and Advocacy Assistant The selected candidate will support global communication, advocacy, and youth engagement efforts. The role combines content creation, social media management, campaign coordination, multimedia production, and knowledge management. Below is a detailed breakdown of the responsibilities. 1. Content Creation and Digital Communications The Communication and Advocacy Assistant will help create engaging and youth-centered communication materials across UNDP platforms. Responsibilities include: Developing timely and visually engaging digital content Supporting UNDP.org youth-related pages Producing social media content Drafting blogs and human-interest stories Designing newsletters and short publications Translating field inputs into compelling digital stories Supporting visual storytelling initiatives Creating reusable Canva templates Ensuring alignment with UNDP branding guidelines The role heavily emphasizes creativity, storytelling, and audience engagement. 2. Graphic Design and Visual Storytelling Visual communication is a major component of this assignment. The selected candidate will: Design social media carousels Create visual cards and infographics Develop presentations and branded templates Produce one-pagers and communication assets Assist with multimedia storytelling Prepare campaign visuals for global observance days Applicants with Canva experience or related design skills will have a strong advantage. 3. Social Media Planning and Engagement The assistant will contribute to UNDP’s youth-focused digital outreach strategy. Tasks include: Preparing social media captions Scheduling content Designing campaign visuals Monitoring social media engagement Identifying youth-related digital trends Supporting youth advocacy campaigns Managing content for international observance days The role requires familiarity with digital engagement strategies and social media communication. 4. Campaigns and Advocacy Support The position also supports global youth advocacy campaigns connected to: The 2030 Agenda Civic participation Digital rights Governance Youth inclusion Peacebuilding The assistant will contribute to: International Youth Day campaigns Webinars and virtual events Regional youth initiatives Advocacy materials Campaign messaging development 5. Multimedia and Production Assistance Candidates will assist in coordinating multimedia production efforts, including: Gathering video footage Supporting storyboarding Assisting editing workflows Coordinating with service providers Creating presentations and visual materials This is ideal for applicants interested in multimedia communication and digital advocacy. 6. Knowledge Management and Documentation The assistant will help maintain communication systems and knowledge products by: Organizing digital communication assets Updating templates and content libraries Managing databases and directories Uploading youth-related content to platforms Supporting learning materials and training resources Strong organizational abilities are essential for success in this role. 7. Event and Youth Engagement Support The selected volunteer will help organize and support: Global youth engagement events Workshops and webinars Outreach campaigns Partner engagement initiatives Youth consultation processes Tasks may include: Designing invitations Taking notes during events Producing summaries Creating event visuals Supporting outreach to youth networks Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements. Education Candidates should possess a: Master’s degree in: Communications Graphic Design Public Relations Journalism Marketing IT or Technology Related fields Required Experience Applicants should have: At least 2 years of relevant experience in: Communications Social media management Advocacy Youth engagement Digital storytelling Content creation Required Skills Strong candidates should demonstrate: Excellent written and oral communication skills Canva or graphic design experience Social media knowledge Digital storytelling abilities Organizational skills Microsoft Office proficiency Ability to manage multiple deadlines Teamwork and multicultural collaboration skills Familiarity with online collaboration tools Desirable Qualifications The following are considered advantages: Experience with youth organizations Understanding of civic engagement Exposure to the UN system Knowledge of development communication Experience in peacebuilding or governance initiatives Familiarity with digital participation initiatives Language Requirements Required English (Fluent) Desirable French Spanish Competencies UNDP is Looking For UNDP seeks candidates who demonstrate: Accountability Creativity Adaptability Professionalism Planning and organization Self-management Sound judgment Flexibility Benefits and Advantages of This Opportunity This opportunity provides several professional and career-building benefits, including: 1. International UN Experience Work directly with a globally recognized UN agency. 2. Remote Work Flexibility Perform the assignment remotely while contributing to global initiatives. 3. Exposure to Global Campaigns Participate in high-level youth advocacy and SDG campaigns. 4. Networking Opportunities Collaborate with UN agencies, youth leaders, governments, and international organizations. 5. Skill Development Strengthen expertise in: Digital communications Advocacy Social media management Graphic design Campaign coordination Multimedia production 6. Contribution to Global Impact Support meaningful youth participation and sustainable development worldwide. Important Notes About the Assignment The assignment is remote The host team is based in Bonn, Germany Volunteer living allowance depends on the volunteer’s country of residence Successful candidates must provide proof of: Citizenship Residency Right to work (if applicable) UNV emphasizes diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity for all applicants. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare: An updated CV or resume Relevant communication or design portfolio Supporting documents demonstrating experience Academic qualifications Applicants are encouraged to apply before the deadline and ensure all required information is complete. Apply here Why This Opportunity Stands Out The UNDP Communication and Advocacy Assistant position is an outstanding opportunity for young communication professionals, digital storytellers, graphic designers, and youth advocates seeking meaningful international experience. The role combines: Creativity Global advocacy Social impact Youth empowerment Digital communication International collaboration For individuals passionate about the SDGs, youth inclusion, governance, and social change, this assignment offers a unique platform to contribute to impactful global initiatives while building a strong international career profile. For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more job opportunities here The post UNDP Communication and Advocacy Assistant Opportunity in Germany (Remote): Fully Detailed Guide for Global Youth Communication Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Southern Africa Trust Recruitment 2026: Apply for Senior Leadership and Operations Roles in Johannesburg
summary: The Southern Africa Trust has announced exciting career opportunities for experienced professionals passionate about inclusive development, governance, and regional impact in Southern Africa. The organisation is currently recruiting for two high-level positions: Head of Programmes Impact and Senior Operations Officer . These positions offer professionals the opportunity to contribute to meaningful social transformation while working with a respected regional organisation dedicated to amplifying the voices of impoverished and excluded communities across Southern Africa. Applications close on 25 May 2026 at 23:59 GMT+2 . About the Southern Africa Trust The Southern Africa Trust works to strengthen the participation and influence of marginalized populations across Southern Africa. Through strategic grantmaking, civic engagement, research, and advocacy, the organisation supports people-centred development and social justice initiatives throughout the region. The Trust is known for promoting inclusivity, collaboration, feminist development principles, and community empowerment. Available Positions 1. Head of Programmes Impact Location: Southern Africa Based (Remote) Contract Type: 3-Year Fixed-Term Contract Reports To: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) The Head of Programmes Impact is a senior leadership role responsible for shaping programme strategy, impact measurement, and regional programme delivery across Southern Africa. Key Responsibilities Lead regional programme strategy and implementation Oversee grantmaking portfolios and impact initiatives Supervise research, learning, and civic infrastructure programmes Support and coordinate Country Managers Ensure alignment, quality, and measurable programme outcomes Ideal Candidate The Southern Africa Trust seeks a strategic and experienced professional with: Strong multi-country programme management experience Expertise in grantmaking and development programming Leadership skills in impact-driven regional initiatives Deep commitment to inclusive and people-centred development To learn more about the role and application details, visit the official organisation website. 2. Senior Operations Officer Location: Johannesburg, South Africa (Hybrid) Contract Type: 3-Year Fixed-Term Contract Reports To: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) The Senior Operations Officer will ensure efficient organisational functioning through strong governance systems, compliance oversight, and operational coordination. Key Responsibilities Manage governance and compliance systems Oversee operational coordination and organisational processes Support risk management and corporate governance Coordinate outsourced operational services Strengthen internal organisational systems Ideal Candidate Applicants should demonstrate: Experience in operations and organisational management Knowledge of governance and compliance frameworks Strong coordination and administrative skills Ability to support efficient institutional systems Further role specifications are available on the official Southern Africa Trust website. What the Southern Africa Trust Is Looking For The organisation is seeking professionals who: Demonstrate integrity, accountability, humility, and collaboration Are committed to feminist and inclusive development values Possess strong professional competence relevant to the role Have legal authorization to work in South Africa The Southern Africa Trust particularly encourages applications from women and young professionals. Benefits of Joining the Southern Africa Trust Successful candidates will benefit from: Competitive remuneration packages Opportunity to work with a respected regional development organisation Meaningful contribution to social justice and inclusion Regional leadership and impact opportunities Flexible work arrangements depending on the role Professional growth in a mission-driven environment Application Deadline Closing Date: 25 May 2026 at 23:59 GMT+2 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the deadline through the official Southern Africa Trust website. For enquiries regarding the application process or roles, applicants may contact: recruitment@southernafricatrust.org The organisation reserves the right to amend the vacancy announcement or decline to make an appointment. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . title: YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Scientists and Climate Advocates
summary: The Youth and Environment Europe (YEE) has officially opened the second round of applications for the second cohort of its Youth Scientific Advisory Board (YSAB) 2026. This exciting opportunity is designed for passionate young scientists, researchers, students, and early-career professionals who want to contribute to environmental protection, climate action, and youth-centered advocacy. The YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board provides a platform for young experts to actively shape environmental policies, scientific advocacy, and sustainability initiatives across Europe and beyond. Selected applicants will work closely on key portfolios including Agrifood Systems, Water Marine Systems, and Climate Justice COP31. Why the YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board Matters Climate change, environmental degradation, and sustainability challenges require strong collaboration between science and advocacy. YEE believes that young experts must be at the center of these conversations. By joining the YSAB, participants will support evidence-based environmental advocacy while ensuring youth voices remain influential in policy discussions and climate solutions. This opportunity is ideal for young professionals who want to: Build international experience in environmental advocacy Collaborate with youth leaders and scientific experts Strengthen research and policy-writing skills Contribute to climate justice initiatives Expand professional networks in sustainability and environmental governance Available Portfolios YEE is specifically recruiting young experts for the following focus areas: Agrifood Systems Applicants interested in sustainable agriculture, food security, regenerative farming, and environmental food systems are encouraged to apply. Water Marine Systems This portfolio focuses on marine conservation, water sustainability, biodiversity protection, and aquatic ecosystem management. Climate Justice COP31 Participants in this portfolio will contribute to climate justice advocacy and youth engagement related to COP31 and global climate negotiations. Benefits of Joining the YSAB Selected members of the Youth Scientific Advisory Board will have the opportunity to: Support Environmental Projects Members will provide updated scientific knowledge and insights on environmental and climate-related topics to strengthen YEE initiatives. Drive Science-Based Advocacy Participants will help ensure YEE’s advocacy work is rooted in credible science while promoting youth-centered approaches. Contribute to Educational Resources YSAB members will assist in developing handbooks, articles, awareness campaigns, and educational materials for young people. Draft Position Papers Participants will contribute to the development of independent, science-backed, and youth-focused policy papers and recommendations. Gain International Exposure This role allows young experts to collaborate with international networks, advocacy groups, and sustainability stakeholders. Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be between 18 and 35 years old Be a student or early-career professional Have a background or strong interest in science, sustainability, environmental studies, climate policy, or related fields Demonstrate interest or expertise in one of the three available portfolios APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board 2026 is: 20 May 2026 at 23:59 CEST Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review the Terms of Reference before submitting their applications. Final Thoughts The YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board 2026 is more than a volunteer or advisory role — it is a chance for young scientists and climate advocates to directly influence environmental action and youth-driven sustainability efforts. If you are passionate about climate advocacy, scientific research, sustainability, or environmental policy, this is an excellent opportunity to make meaningful contributions while building your international experience and professional network. Because science and advocacy go hand in hand when securing a sustainable future. The post YEE Youth Scientific Advisory Board 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Scientists and Climate Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Milestone Trial LawHER Scholarship 2026–2027: Win Up to $25,000 to Advance Your Legal Career
summary: The Milestone Trial LawHER Scholarship is now accepting applications for the 2026–2027 academic year, offering financial support to ambitious women pursuing careers as plaintiff trial lawyers in the United States. Created to empower the next generation of women in litigation, the scholarship program will award one $25,000 scholarship and two additional $10,000 scholarships to outstanding law students who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, and a commitment to advancing diversity in the legal profession. The initiative is administered by Scholarship America, one of the nation’s leading scholarship management organizations, and aims to support women who are preparing to make a lasting impact in plaintiff trial law. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive substantial educational funding that can be used for a wide range of academic expenses. Awards include: One scholarship worth $25,000 Two scholarships worth $10,000 each Potential internship opportunities during law school Possible career opportunities with Milestone after graduation Funding applicable to tuition and other education-related expenses Although the scholarship is non-renewable, eligible students may reapply in future application cycles. Who Is Eligible? To qualify for the Milestone Trial LawHER Scholarship, applicants must: Be currently enrolled in a 2L or 3L law school program Intend to become a plaintiff trial lawyer Plan to attend an ABA-accredited law school full-time during the upcoming academic year Be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident Self-identify as female Maintain a minimum GPA of 3.5 on a 4.0 scale Please note that Milestone employees and their immediate family members are not eligible to apply. Required Application Materials Applicants must submit the following materials electronically before the deadline: 1. Official Academic Transcript The transcript must include: Student name Institution name Course grades Credit hours Academic terms completed Grade reports are not accepted. 2. Written Essay Applicants must submit an essay of approximately 1,500 words addressing the following topic: “How is legal technology empowering women trial lawyers to better represent the Bar, better represent their clients, and advance diversity in Law?” The essay must also include a short note confirming that the applicant is the primary creator of both the written and video essay submissions. 3. Video Essay Applicants will also be evaluated based on a video essay response as part of the final selection process. Selection Criteria Finalists will be evaluated based on: Academic achievement Leadership experience Community involvement Work experience Educational and career goals Personal or family circumstances Recipients will then be selected based on the strength of their written and video essay responses. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Important Dates Applications Open: Currently Open Application Deadline: May 27, 2026 at 3:00 PM CT Recipient Notification: Late July 2026 Scholarship Payment Distribution: August 2026 Applicants are encouraged to use a personal email address rather than a school or work email to ensure continued access to notifications. Why This Scholarship Matters Women remain underrepresented in plaintiff trial law, with the American Association for Justice reporting that only 19% of the plaintiff bar are women. The Milestone Trial LawHER Scholarship seeks to bridge this gap by investing in talented future litigators who are committed to advancing representation, innovation, and equity within the legal profession. For aspiring women trial attorneys, this scholarship provides more than financial support — it offers recognition, visibility, and potential career-building opportunities in a competitive legal field. Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their essays and supporting materials early to avoid last-minute submission issues before the deadline. title: Global Majority AI Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (Fully Funded)
summary: Applications are now open for the Global Majority AI Fellowship 2026, an exciting fully funded opportunity for civil society experts from the Global South to participate in the first-ever United Nations Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva, Switzerland, taking place from July 6–7, 2026. The fellowship is organized by the Heinrich Böll Foundation Washington, DC and aims to amplify underrepresented voices in global conversations on artificial intelligence governance, digital rights, human rights, climate justice, and ethical technology policy. Selected fellows will receive full travel support, accommodation, and access to one of the most important emerging global platforms shaping the future of AI governance. About the Global Majority AI Fellowship Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming economies, governance systems, labor markets, and societies worldwide. However, many current AI governance frameworks are still heavily influenced by a small group of countries and large technology corporations. To address this imbalance, the United Nations established the Global Dialogue on AI Governance, a recurring multilateral forum designed to ensure that AI governance reflects the priorities and realities of all nations, especially those from underrepresented regions. The first session of the Global Dialogue will take place in Geneva, Switzerland, from July 6–7, 2026, alongside the ITU Summit and the WSIS Forum. Through this fellowship, the Heinrich Böll Foundation seeks to strengthen Global South participation in these critical discussions and create pathways for more inclusive, equitable, and human rights-based AI governance. Fellowship Focus Areas The Global Dialogue on AI Governance will focus on major thematic areas, including: AI opportunities and societal implications Economic, ethical, cultural, linguistic, and technical dimensions of AI Bridging global AI divides through digital access and capacity building Safe, secure, and trustworthy AI systems Human rights, transparency, accountability, and human oversight in AI governance Participants will also contribute perspectives related to: Environmental sustainability Labor and economic justice Bias and discrimination in AI systems Conflict and digital governance Gender balance in global AI policy spaces Benefits of the Global Majority AI Fellowship Successful applicants will receive: Fully funded international travel to Geneva Airport transportation support Up to three nights of accommodation Conference registration and accreditation Access to global networking opportunities Visibility through the Heinrich Böll Foundation’s communication channels Opportunities to engage with global policymakers, researchers, and civil society leaders Fellowship Objectives The program aims to: Increase Global South representation in international AI governance discussions Support learning and networking among emerging and established leaders Build stronger advocacy and policy engagement on digital governance issues Facilitate collaboration between civil society actors working on AI and social justice Expectations from Fellows Selected fellows will be expected to: Attend the UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva from July 6–7, 2026 Participate in fellowship-related activities before and after the event Submit a short statement outlining key AI governance concerns Share reflections on their experience Engage actively on social media throughout the fellowship period Contribute to a growing international network of AI governance advocates Eligibility Criteria Applicants should: Be based in countries receiving Official Development Assistance (ODA) Have experience or demonstrated interest in AI governance, digital rights, climate justice, data justice, or related fields Be available to travel to Geneva in July 2026 Apply both to the official UN AI Dialogue 2026 Call for Participation Support and to the Heinrich Böll Foundation Washington, DC Required Application Materials Applicants must submit: 1. Resume/CV Maximum recommended length: 2 pages Include country of residence and gender Avoid including photographs or marital status 2. Motivational Statement Applicants should write a maximum 300-word reflection addressing: The biggest challenge facing AI governance today Proposed solutions and current work being done How participation in the Global Dialogue would advance their goals Application Deadline Applications are accepted on a rolling basis. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as soon as possible because selection may close once available spots are filled. How to Apply Candidates must: Apply to the official UN AI Dialogue 2026 Call for Participation Support Send their application materials to the Heinrich Böll Foundation Washington, DC via: Drew.Mitnick@us.boell.org Mariama.Balde@us.boell.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . title: Research Fellow (Home Office) at Africa Policy Research Institute (APRI): Just Green Technology Transition Programme – Full Opportunity Guide
summary: The Africa Policy Research Institute (APRI) is offering an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and research-driven professional to join its Just Green Technology Transition Programme as a Research Fellow . This is a home-office-based role designed for experts in political economy, economics, energy policy, or industrial policy who are passionate about shaping Africa’s green industrial future. The position focuses on bridging the gap between policy, financing mechanisms, and firm-level realities within African energy transition and industrial development systems. The selected Fellow will play a central role in producing high-quality research, engaging stakeholders across continents, and co-developing actionable policy frameworks that support African-led green industrialisation. This is a 12-month full-time engagement with competitive remuneration, travel opportunities, and 30 vacation days. About APRI (Africa Policy Research Institute) APRI is an independent, nonpartisan policy institute that focuses on African development issues. It contributes to shaping German, European Union, and African policy debates by producing research outputs such as: Policy briefs Analytical reports Short research papers Strategic policy recommendations APRI’s mission is to center African perspectives in global policy discussions , especially in areas such as energy transition, industrial policy, trade, and geopolitics. Key Responsibilities of the Research Fellow The Research Fellow will be responsible for leading and contributing to high-level policy research and stakeholder engagement activities. Key duties include: 1. Policy and Strategy Research Conduct in-depth research on African industrial and energy strategies Analyze policy instruments across African economies focusing on green energy and industrialisation Study cooperation frameworks between Africa and global partners 2. Financing and Partnership Analysis Evaluate international financing mechanisms and technology partnerships Assess alignment between external investments and African industrial priorities Study policy impact across macro, meso, and micro levels 3. Case Study Development Produce detailed case studies of African firms, startups, and OEMs involved in green technology Ground policy recommendations in real market and industry data 4. Stakeholder Engagement Organize and facilitate high-level roundtables and policy dialogues Engage stakeholders from Africa, Europe, and Asia Build cross-regional expert networks for sustained collaboration 5. Research Outputs and Publications Draft policy briefs and research publications with actionable recommendations Contribute to donor reporting, monitoring, and evaluation documentation Collaborate with international research partners on joint outputs Required Qualifications Essential Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Advanced degree (Master’s or PhD) in: Political Economy Economics Energy Policy Industrial Policy 4–6 years of relevant professional experience in: Policy research Think tanks Development research or related fields Strong focus on African economic development or energy transition Proven experience in qualitative research methods: Policy analysis Document review Semi-structured interviews Case study research Strong writing skills (policy briefs, reports, or articles) Experience engaging policymakers, diplomats, or industry actors Fluency in English Desirable Qualifications Knowledge of international green industrial and energy partnerships Experience with think tanks or international organizations Existing networks in African ministries, firms, or industry bodies Familiarity with EU–Africa energy and industrial policy relations Key Competencies Strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills Ability to connect policy frameworks with real-world implementation Experience working in cross-regional research environments Strong facilitation skills for workshops and high-level discussions Commitment to African-centered policy perspectives Employment Terms Duration: 12 months Work type: Full-time, home office Benefits: Competitive remuneration Travel opportunities 30 vacation days CLICK HERE TO APPLY Important note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Due to high application volumes, applicants are advised not to send follow-up inquiries. Deadline No specific application deadline has been provided in the listing. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . title: IC Wealth Opens National Service Opportunities for Graduates in Accounting, Finance, Computer Science, Economics, and Humanities
summary: IC Wealth has announced exciting National Service opportunities for qualified graduates who are looking to gain practical industry experience and build successful professional careers. The company is currently inviting applications from National Service Personnel with academic backgrounds in Accounting, Banking Finance, Computer Science, Economics, and Humanities to join its growing team. This opportunity offers graduates the chance to work in a professional environment where they can develop industry-relevant skills, gain hands-on experience, and contribute meaningfully to organizational growth. Applicants who are passionate about career development, learning, and professional excellence are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. About IC Wealth IC Wealth is recognized for providing financial and professional services that support businesses and individuals across various sectors. As part of its commitment to talent development and youth empowerment, the organization is opening its doors to National Service Personnel who are eager to gain workplace exposure and professional mentorship. The recruitment drive is designed to help graduates transition smoothly from academic life into the professional world while equipping them with practical skills that can improve their long-term career prospects. Available National Service Opportunities IC Wealth is currently encouraging applications from graduates with educational backgrounds in the following fields: Accounting Banking Finance Computer Science Economics Humanities Candidates from these disciplines will have the opportunity to work with professionals, participate in organizational projects, and build valuable workplace experience that can strengthen their future employment opportunities. Why You Should Apply Joining IC Wealth as a National Service Personnel can provide several important career benefits, including: 1. Professional Work Experience Applicants will gain real-world industry experience that can help them become more competitive in the job market after completing their national service. 2. Career Development Opportunities The opportunity allows graduates to improve their communication, analytical, teamwork, and problem-solving skills within a professional environment. 3. Networking and Mentorship Working alongside experienced professionals can help National Service Personnel build valuable professional connections and receive mentorship that supports career growth. 4. Exposure to Diverse Industries Graduates will gain insight into financial services, technology, administration, and corporate operations depending on their academic background and assigned responsibilities. 5. Workplace Readiness The experience can help young professionals understand workplace expectations, improve productivity, and develop confidence in their chosen career paths. Who Can Apply? IC Wealth is seeking applications from: National Service Personnel Recent graduates Individuals with backgrounds in Accounting, Banking Finance, Computer Science, Economics, or Humanities Candidates who are willing to learn and work in a professional environment Applicants interested in career growth and professional development Application Deadline Applicants are advised to submit their applications before the stated deadline: Application Deadline: 30th May 2026 Early applications may have an advantage during the recruitment process, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their Applications and CVs via email to: careers@ic.africa Applicants are encouraged to ensure that their CVs are updated and professionally prepared before submission. Tips for a Strong Application To improve your chances of being selected, consider the following tips before applying: Update your CV with relevant academic achievements and skills Highlight internship experience, leadership roles, or volunteer activities Use a professional email address Proofread your application before submission Clearly indicate your field of study and National Service status Final Thoughts The IC Wealth National Service recruitment presents an excellent opportunity for graduates to gain valuable professional experience while building a strong foundation for future career success. Whether your background is in Finance, Computer Science, Economics, Accounting, or Humanities, this opportunity can help you develop practical skills, expand your professional network, and prepare for long-term career growth. Interested candidates should ensure they submit their applications before 30th May 2026 to be considered for the opportunity. For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more job opportunities here.
